data.name: DirectChildRuleTitle
data.value: LVGLSharp layouts should use FlowLayoutPanel row containers

data.name: DirectChildRuleMessage
data.value: Add a FlowLayoutPanel row container to the main TableLayoutPanel instead of '{0}'

data.name: DirectChildRuleDescription
data.value: LVGLSharp layouts should add one FlowLayoutPanel per main table row and place the actual controls inside that flow container.

data.name: AbsoluteRowRuleTitle
data.value: LVGLSharp layouts should use absolute table row heights

data.name: AbsoluteRowRuleMessage
data.value: Use an absolute RowStyle height instead of SizeType.Percent in LVGLSharp layouts

data.name: AbsoluteRowRuleDescription
data.value: LVGLSharp layout uses fixed row heights for the outer main TableLayoutPanel to keep LVGL controls visible.

data.name: MissingRuntimeRuleTitle
data.value: LVGLSharp.Forms requires a platform runtime package

data.name: MissingRuntimeRuleMessage
data.value: Add a reference to LVGLSharp.Runtime.Windows or LVGLSharp.Runtime.Linux so LVGLSharp.Forms can register a runtime automatically.

data.name: MissingRuntimeRuleDescription
data.value: LVGLSharp.Forms needs a platform runtime package reference to provide the host window implementation and automatic runtime registration.

data.name: RemoteDemoNonGlobalUsingRuleTitle
data.value: Remote demos should use project-level global usings for LVGLSharp namespaces

data.name: RemoteDemoNonGlobalUsingRuleMessage
data.value: Move '{0}' to a project-level global using so the remote demo follows the shared LVGLSharp namespace style.

data.name: RemoteDemoNonGlobalUsingRuleDescription
data.value: Remote demos should declare LVGLSharp namespaces once at the project level, for example with <Using Include="..." />, instead of repeating file-level using directives.

data.name: RemoteDemoQualifiedNameRuleTitle
data.value: Remote demos should rely on global usings instead of fully qualified LVGLSharp type names

data.name: RemoteDemoQualifiedNameRuleMessage
data.value: Replace fully qualified '{0}' with the shared project-level global using style in this remote demo.

data.name: RemoteDemoQualifiedNameRuleDescription
data.value: Remote demos should use project-level LVGLSharp global usings so code can refer to forms, drawing, and runtime types without fully qualifying each name.
